title: Chilli Soya
tagline: Restaurant-style Indo-Chinese chilli with air-fried (not deep-fried) soya chunks in a glossy soy-MSG sauce.
servingsNote: Soya IS the protein base here — no extra needed
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (150 g dry soya chunks base).
Genuinely one of the best protein-to-calorie ratios you can cook — ~40 g protein for ~400 kcal a serving, because soya chunks are the base (no need to add more protein).

Air-frying instead of the classic cornstarch deep-fry keeps it lean; if you skip the cornstarch coating entirely and just spice-rub + spray oil, you save another ~100 kcal across the batch but lose some crispness.
MSG is doing real work here for the restaurant umami — ~1/4 tsp (≈1–1.25 g) for this batch is plenty and well within normal cooking limits; don't overdo it or it turns sharp.

items: 1 tbsp oil (helps them crisp in the air fryer)
2–2.5 tbsp dark soy sauce (no light soy)
1/4 tsp salt (to make up for skipping light soy)
Cornflour slurry: 2 tsp cornflour + 3–4 tbsp water

steps.title: Boil & squeeze
steps.body: Boil 150 g dry soya chunks until soft, then squeeze out ALL the excess water — this is the key step; skip it and they turn soggy and won't crisp.

steps.title: Coat
steps.body: Toss the squeezed chunks with 2 tbsp cornstarch, 1 tbsp maida, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper and 1 tbsp oil. The oil is what lets them crisp in the air fryer without frying.

steps.title: Air-fry
steps.body: Preheat the air fryer to 180°C for 3–4 minutes. Spread the coated chunks in a single layer (don't overcrowd) and air-fry at 180°C for 10–15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway. For extra crisp, bump to 200°C for the last 2–3 minutes. Without the coating they'd just dry out and go chewy.

steps.title: Start the sauce base
steps.body: Heat a wok on high, add 1 tbsp oil, and when hot add 1 tbsp ginger, 1 tbsp garlic, 1–2 green chillies, 2 tbsp coriander stems and the spring onion bulbs. Stir a few seconds.

steps.title: Add the veg
steps.body: Add the onion and capsicum squares and stir-fry on high for a minute — keep them crunchy, not soft.

steps.title: Build & season the sauce
steps.body: Add 2–2.5 tbsp dark soy sauce plus 1/4 tsp salt (standing in for light soy), 1/3 cup hot water, 1.5–2 tbsp vinegar, 1–1.5 tsp sugar, 1/2 tsp white pepper and 1/4 tsp MSG. Bring to a boil. Add the MSG here with the other sauces so it dissolves evenly.

steps.title: Thicken
steps.body: Stir the cornflour slurry (2 tsp cornflour + 3–4 tbsp water) and add it gradually, stirring, until the sauce turns glossy and coats a spoon.

steps.title: Toss & serve
steps.body: Add the air-fried soya chunks and toss quickly to coat — do this fast so they keep their crunch. Taste and adjust with a splash more soy + a pinch of salt if it needs depth (not more MSG). Garnish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

notes: No light soy sauce? Dark soy alone gives colour and a touch of sweetness but is less salty — use it plus a pinch of extra salt.
Don't double the sauces linearly if you scale the soya up — increase by ~30–40% and taste-adjust at the end, or it gets too salty.
Toss the crisped soya into the sauce at the very last moment; sitting in the sauce softens the coating.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
